extraction.one_line_summary: DEMAR Arnold won sprint finish in Messina after Groupama-FDJ controlled climb to distance pure sprinters Cavendish and Ewan.
tactical_patterns: Groupama-FDJ deliberately raised tempo on 20km 4% climb specifically to eliminate Cavendish/Ewan, succeeding within 10km.
SWIFT Ben tactically took intermediate sprint bonus seconds 11km out, denying GC riders meaningful time gaps.
SCOTSON Miles won corner positioning race under 1km for Groupama, critical on technical twisting finish with headwind straight.

rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: dropped first on controlled 4% climb, never regained contact, confirms vulnerability even moderate gradients
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: isolated with one helper on climb, over 2min deficit, worse climbing form than Cavendish
rider_signals.DEMAR Arnold: positive: team executed elimination climb tactic then won from unfavorable boxed position in headwind
rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: neutral: another top-5 but boxed on one side in finale, accumulating consistency without winning
rider_signals.SWIFT Ben: positive: aggressive intermediate sprint positioning denied GC riders time bonuses, intelligent racing for teammate protection
